title: Pediatric CDS Review Record
description: EP7-T1 (FR-WP7-01). DATA CONTRACT ONLY: this schema shapes a review record for a proposed change to any governed knowledge-base artifact (a rule, a candidate, a manifest field, etc.) as it moves through `proposed` -> `under-review` -> (optionally) `disputed` -> `approved`/`rejected`. It does not define, and this repository does not yet ship, any review-portal application, API, database, or server that produces or consumes these records â€” that surface is described (never implemented) by the EP7-T2 design doc. `approvedBy` is deliberately shaped to be byte-for-byte compatible with schemas/module-manifest.schema.json's `approvedBy` and schemas/rule.schema.json's `clinicalApprovers`: a flat array of non-empty strings. That compatibility is the point â€” a future, SEPARATELY GATED act could copy an approved review record's `approvedBy[]` verbatim into a module manifest once this project deliberately raises that field's `maxItems: 0` ceiling; this schema does not raise it and does not perform that act. D-4 (no synthetic review may ever populate an approver field) is enforced here structurally, not by convention: every entry in `reviewers[]` is pinned to `reviewerType: {"const": "human"}` and `attestedHuman: {"const": true}`, which makes it schema-impossible to represent an ARC council run, a `council-review`/`rf` verification bundle, an automated linter, or a model's own self-review as a reviewer or an approver â€” there is no enum value, no field, no shape in this schema that such a synthetic source could occupy. If ARC/automated output is ever consulted as an input, it belongs only in free-text narrative fields (`changeProposal.rationale`, `history[].note`, `conflictResolution.reason`/`notes`) as advisory context, never as a `reviewers[]` entry and never contributing to `approvedBy[]`. Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, or that a named human clinician reviewed the underlying clinical content â€” see clinicalApprovers/approvedBy's own descriptions for that standing caveat.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer review-record-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes.

id.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Review record identifier, e.g. `RR-2026-001`. Distinct from `changeProposal.proposalId` â€” one proposal has exactly one review record, but the two ids are tracked separately so a review record can be referenced independently of the proposal it reviews.

reviewers.description: REQUIRED key. `[]` only while `workflowState` is `proposed` (no review has started yet); every other state requires at least the two reviewers of the dual-review step (`minItems: 2`, enforced below by the per-state `allOf`/`if`/`then`). Every item is a named human reviewer â€” see `$defs/humanReviewer` for why an automated or ARC-sourced entry cannot be expressed here at all.

triggeredAt.description: REQUIRED, non-null when this object is present. When the dual reviewers' decisions diverged and the record entered `disputed`.

reason.description: REQUIRED, non-null when this object is present. Plain-language statement of what the reviewers disagreed about.

resolvedBy.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable while the dispute is still open (`workflowState: disputed` and unresolved). MUST be a non-null `$defs/humanIdentity` once `workflowState` is `approved` or `rejected` (enforced below) â€” a conflict can only be closed out by a real named human, never by an automated arbiter, exactly as D-4 requires for every other approval-adjacent field in this schema.

resolution.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable while unresolved; a closed enum once set.

notes.description: REQUIRED key, freely nullable. Additional narrative context; may reference advisory ARC/automated input as long as it is never mistaken for a reviewer or approver (see this schema's top-level description).

approvedBy.description: REQUIRED key. Mirrors schemas/module-manifest.schema.json's `approvedBy` and schemas/rule.schema.json's `clinicalApprovers` shape EXACTLY: a flat array of non-empty strings. `[]` in every state except `approved` (enforced below by the per-state `allOf`/`if`/`then`); `minItems: 2` once `approved`, one string per qualifying entry in `reviewers[]` whose `decision` is `approve` (dual sign-off). Never populate this field from an ARC run id, a model name, or any string not corresponding 1:1 to a `reviewers[]` entry that carries `reviewerType: "human"` and `attestedHuman: true` â€” this schema cannot cross-check that correspondence by content (no `$data` support in this repository's zero-dependency validator), so the guarantee here is: (a) structural, via the state-conditioned `maxItems: 0`/`minItems: 2` gates and the `reviewers[]` `contains`/`minContains` check tying approval to human sign-off, and (b) procedural, via the same review process this record documents. Raising the sibling schemas' `maxItems: 0` ceiling to accept these strings into a module manifest is a separate, deliberate, reviewable act that this schema does not perform.

history.description: REQUIRED key. Append-only log of every workflow-state transition this record has passed through, oldest first. The first entry's `state` MUST be `proposed` (a review record is always created in the `proposed` state).

createdAt.description: REQUIRED, non-null. When this review record was created (always coincides with the first `history[]` entry).

updatedAt.description: REQUIRED, non-null. When this review record was last modified (always coincides with the last `history[]` entry).

workflowState.description: Closed enum, FR-WP7-02. `proposed` = change-proposal authored, no review started. `under-review` = dual review in progress. `disputed` = the two reviewers' decisions diverged and a `conflictResolution` is open. `approved`/`rejected` = terminal states.

targetArtifacts.description: Repo-relative paths of the governed artifact(s) this proposal would change, e.g. `modules/anemia/rules.json`.

proposedBy.description: Free-text identification of who/what authored the proposal. D-4 restricts only APPROVAL sources (`reviewers[]`/`approvedBy[]`), not proposal authorship â€” a proposal may legitimately originate from a human, a codemod, or an automated evidence pipeline; that provenance is recorded here as plain text, never as a reviewer or approver.

rationale.description: Plain-language justification, expected to reference evidence ids where applicable. Advisory ARC/automated review output, if consulted, may be summarized here as narrative context only â€” never as a `reviewers[]` entry or an `approvedBy[]` source.

reviewerRole.description: Minimal role vocabulary needed to model dual-review plus conflict-resolution. The full role/seat vocabulary (including how it cross-references, and explicitly excludes, ARC's council seat taxonomy as an approval source) is authored in the EP7-T2 review-portal design doc, not here â€” this schema only needs enough structure to keep `reviewers[]` well-typed.

name.description: The reviewer's real full name. Never a system, tool, or run identifier â€” see `reviewerType`.

credential.description: The reviewer's professional credential/license (e.g. `MD`, `DO`, `RN, CPNP`, `PharmD`). REQUIRED and non-empty for every reviewer; this is the human-identity field D-4 requires.

reviewerType.description: Pinned to the single legal value `"human"` â€” there is no other enum member this field could ever hold. This is the structural guarantee: an ARC council run, an `rf`/`council-review` verification bundle, an automated linter, or a model's self-review CANNOT be expressed as a `reviewers[]` entry, because no value other than `"human"` validates here. Mirrors the same fail-closed pattern schemas/rule.schema.json (`clinicalApprovers` `maxItems: 0`) and schemas/module-manifest.schema.json (`approvedBy` `maxItems: 0`) use for the same guardrail (D-4/AC-D4).

decision.description: `pending` while assigned but not yet decided (legitimate during `under-review`); a closed enum of terminal per-reviewer decisions otherwise.

attestedHuman.description: Pinned to the literal `true` â€” an explicit, separate attestation flag alongside `reviewerType`, so a populated reviewer record always carries two independent structural markers of human identity, not just one.

decidedAt.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable while `decision` is `pending`; a well-formed date-time once a decision has been recorded.

actor.description: Who/what caused this transition (a human reviewer's name, or a system actor such as `system:proposal-intake` for the initial `proposed` entry).

note.description: REQUIRED key, freely nullable. Optional narrative context for this transition.
